medical_history.chief_complaint: 1 year history of lethargy, nausea and pre-syncopal symptoms
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented with symptoms consistent with SIADH and no sino-nasal symptoms. Routine blood investigations revealed significant hyponatraemia, with a serum sodium level of 111 mmol/L. Diagnosis of SIADH was made based on being euvolaemic, euthyroid and having normal adrenal function.

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Returned to normal after removal of the tumour

radiographic_examinations.findings: Normal pituitary. Incidental right maxillary sinus high-signal intensity lesion seen on contrast-enhanced T1 sequences
Soft tissue density lesion within the right maxillary sinus with areas of presumed calcification
High-activity lesion with no regional or distant disease

other_tests.findings: Pedunculated lesion arising from the roof of the maxillary sinus

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with symptoms consistent with SIADH and no sino-nasal symptoms. MRI revealed an isolated lesion in the right maxillary sinus, and biopsy confirmed esthesioneuroblastoma. The resolution of SIADH symptoms post-resection supported the diagnosis.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: complete right spheno-ethmoidectomy, middle turbinectomy, access septoplasty, right endoscopic medial maxillectomy, excision en bloc along with partial resection of the orbital floor, medial orbital wall and surrounding mucosa of the maxillary sinus
